commentary_text: The model rates Ecuador as a heavy favorite at 55%, while the sportsbook consensus pulls back to 40%—a 15-percentage-point gap on the Ecuador win. The market also shortens the Ivory Coast win from 17% to 27%, suggesting bookmakers see meaningful uncertainty that the Elo-based model does not capture. Recent form, injury news, or lineup changes affecting either squad could explain why real-money pricing hedges more cautiously than the statistical baseline. The dashboard flags this spread as a signal for further investigation rather than a judgment on which assessment proves more accurate.
